description: Rust/WASM acceleration coverage manifest for tsb core functions
notes: Every value export from src/core/index.ts, plus every top-level value export from src/index.ts whose source is under src/core/**, is classified as either rust-wasm (implemented in Rust, exported through WASM, wired into the accelerated path) or ts-only-ineligible (not suitable for Rust/WASM with a concrete, documented reason). There are no unclassified or todo entries. The coverage check script (scripts/wasm-coverage-check.ts) validates this manifest against the live export surface on every run.

entries.name: natSorted
entries.notes: Accelerated path covers string arrays without a key function. Calls with a key function fall back to the TypeScript implementation automatically.

entries.name: natSortKey
entries.reason: Return type is readonly (string|number)[] — a JS-specific union array that has no direct WASM representation without per-element boxing. The resulting round-trip cost would likely exceed any benefit.

entries.name: searchsorted
entries.notes: Accelerated path covers numeric (f64) and string arrays with the default comparator. Calls with a custom compareFn or a sorter fall back to TypeScript automatically.

entries.name: argsortScalars
entries.notes: Accelerated path covers numeric and string arrays with the default comparator. Custom compareFn falls back to TypeScript.

entries.name: Index
entries.reason: Complex JS class with JS-specific semantics: symbol-keyed properties, JS object identity, generator methods, and deep integration with the JS iterator protocol. All label-lookup logic depends on JS Map/Set identity.

entries.name: RangeIndex
entries.reason: Inherits from Index (JS class). Its primary value is lazy range generation using JS generators. No CPU-heavy numeric kernel to accelerate independently of the class.

entries.name: Dtype
entries.reason: JS class whose instances are used as JS runtime type tokens. Equality is checked via JS object identity. Cannot be serialised to/from WASM without losing identity semantics.

entries.name: Series
entries.reason: High-level JS class whose methods accept arbitrary JS callbacks, return new Series instances, and interoperate with JS accessors, Index objects, and the extension registry. Porting at the class level would require replacing the entire TS implementation.

entries.name: DataFrame
entries.reason: Same as Series: high-level JS class with arbitrary JS callbacks, JS accessors, rolling/expanding/ewm subclasses, and deep interoperation with Series and Index.

entries.name: DataFrameRolling
entries.reason: Inner class of DataFrame; window computation returns new DataFrame/Series instances and accepts JS callbacks.

entries.name: DataFrameExpanding
entries.reason: Inner class of DataFrame; same reasoning as DataFrameRolling.

entries.name: DataFrameEwm
entries.reason: Inner class of DataFrame; exponentially weighted window — depends on JS class state and returns DataFrame/Series.

entries.name: StringAccessor
entries.reason: JS accessor class exposing string operations that use JS regex and Intl APIs internally. Many operations have no WASM equivalent without reimplementing the JS Unicode/Intl stack.

entries.name: DatetimeAccessor
entries.reason: Wraps JS Date arithmetic and timezone conversions. Correctness depends on the JS Temporal/Intl system which cannot be reproduced from WASM without calling back into JS.

entries.name: CategoricalAccessor
entries.reason: JS accessor that manages a categorical code/categories array pair stored as JS arrays with JS object identity. No isolated numeric kernel.

entries.name: MultiIndex
entries.reason: JS class holding multiple JS Index objects; label lookup uses JS Map with tuple keys represented as strings. Deep JS-identity dependency.

entries.name: Interval
entries.reason: JS class whose endpoints can be arbitrary Scalar values (including JS Date and Timedelta instances). Cannot be serialised to WASM without losing JS object semantics.

entries.name: IntervalIndex
entries.reason: Inherits from Index; holds Interval instances. Same JS-identity constraint as Interval.

entries.name: CategoricalIndex
entries.reason: JS class for categorical index labels; depends on JS Map/Set identity for deduplication.

entries.name: Period
entries.reason: JS class wrapping a period value with frequency metadata. Arithmetic calls JS Date methods and the date-offset registry.

entries.name: PeriodIndex
entries.reason: Holds Period instances; inherits from Index. Same JS-identity dependency as Period.

entries.name: Timedelta
entries.reason: JS class wrapping a duration in milliseconds plus component accessors. Arithmetic depends on JS Date. No isolated WASM-friendly kernel.

entries.name: TimedeltaIndex
entries.reason: Holds Timedelta instances; inherits from Index. Same JS dependency as Timedelta.

entries.name: timedelta_range
entries.reason: Generates a TimedeltaIndex using JS Date arithmetic. Output is a JS class instance array.

entries.name: Day
entries.reason: JS DateOffset subclass; applies/rolls dates using JS Date. Cannot function independently of the JS date system.

entries.name: Hour
entries.reason: JS DateOffset subclass; same reasoning as Day.

entries.name: Minute
entries.reason: JS DateOffset subclass; same reasoning as Day.

entries.name: Second
entries.reason: JS DateOffset subclass; same reasoning as Day.

entries.name: Milli
entries.reason: JS DateOffset subclass; same reasoning as Day.

entries.name: Week
entries.reason: JS DateOffset subclass; same reasoning as Day.

entries.name: MonthEnd
entries.reason: JS DateOffset subclass; end-of-month rollforward requires JS Date.setFullYear/setMonth.

entries.name: MonthBegin
entries.reason: JS DateOffset subclass; same reasoning as MonthEnd.

entries.name: YearEnd
entries.reason: JS DateOffset subclass; same reasoning as MonthEnd.

entries.name: YearBegin
entries.reason: JS DateOffset subclass; same reasoning as MonthEnd.

entries.name: BusinessDay
entries.reason: JS DateOffset subclass; business-day skipping requires day-of-week arithmetic via JS Date.

entries.name: DatetimeIndex
entries.reason: Holds JS Date timestamps; inherits from Index. All construction and arithmetic uses JS Date APIs.

entries.name: date_range
entries.reason: Returns a DatetimeIndex generated by iterated JS Date arithmetic with date-offset objects.

entries.name: bdate_range
entries.reason: Business-day date range; depends on JS Date weekday computation and the DateOffset registry.

entries.name: resolveFreq
entries.reason: Parses a frequency string and returns a JS DateOffset instance from the runtime registry.

entries.name: TZDatetimeIndex
entries.reason: Holds timezone-aware JS Date values; localisation and conversion delegate entirely to the JS Intl.DateTimeFormat API.

entries.name: tz_localize
entries.reason: Creates a TZDatetimeIndex using JS Intl APIs; cannot run inside WASM.

entries.name: tz_convert
entries.reason: Converts between timezones via JS Intl.DateTimeFormat; same Intl dependency as tz_localize.

entries.name: Timestamp
entries.reason: JS wrapper around a nanosecond-precision timestamp. Format and parse operations delegate to JS Intl and Date. Object identity used throughout tsb's datetime pipeline.

entries.name: dataFrameAssign
entries.reason: Accepts an AssignSpec object whose values can be arbitrary JS functions (column transformers). Cannot be represented in WASM without callback round-trips.

entries.name: reindexSeries
entries.reason: Operates on Series and Index JS class instances; fill methods (ffill/bfill) are JS-level operations on the resulting arrays. Porting requires migrating the entire Series/Index stack.

entries.name: reindexDataFrame
entries.reason: Same as reindexSeries; additionally accepts optional fill_value which may be a JS object.

entries.name: alignSeries
entries.reason: Returns a pair of re-indexed Series instances; depends entirely on JS class instances and the reindex pipeline.

entries.name: alignDataFrame
entries.reason: Same as alignSeries for DataFrames.

entries.name: insertColumn
entries.reason: Mutates a DataFrame JS object's column map. The DataFrame is a live JS instance; mutation is an in-place JS operation with no WASM-amenable equivalent.

entries.name: popColumn
entries.reason: Same as insertColumn; removes a column from the DataFrame's internal JS Map.

entries.name: reorderColumns
entries.reason: Reorders the JS Map entries in a DataFrame; depends on JS object mutation.

entries.name: moveColumn
entries.reason: Combines reorder and insert; same JS object mutation dependency.

entries.name: dataFrameFromPairs
entries.reason: Constructs a DataFrame from an array of [label, Series] pairs; returns a new JS DataFrame instance.

entries.name: toDictOriented
entries.reason: Serialises a DataFrame to a plain JS object with arbitrary key/value structure. Output type is a JS Record whose shape depends on the orient parameter.

entries.name: fromDictOriented
entries.reason: Deserialises a plain JS object to a DataFrame; input is an untyped JS object whose structure varies by orient.

entries.name: getAttrs
entries.reason: Returns a reference to the JS Attrs object (plain Record). Semantics are based on JS object identity; no numeric computation involved.

entries.name: setAttrs
entries.reason: Sets the attrs object on a Series/DataFrame using JS object identity. Pure JS metadata operation.

entries.name: updateAttrs
entries.reason: Merges an attrs object using Object.assign semantics; pure JS.

entries.name: copyAttrs
entries.reason: Shallow-copies an attrs Record; pure JS object operation.

entries.name: withAttrs
entries.reason: Returns a new Series/DataFrame with updated attrs; depends on JS class constructor.

entries.name: clearAttrs
entries.reason: Removes the attrs property; pure JS object mutation.

entries.name: hasAttrs
entries.reason: Checks for the attrs property presence; pure JS property check.

entries.name: getAttr
entries.reason: Key-value lookup in a JS object; pure JS property access.

entries.name: setAttr
entries.reason: Key-value write in a JS object; pure JS property mutation.

entries.name: deleteAttr
entries.reason: Deletes a key from a JS object; pure JS property mutation.

entries.name: attrsCount
entries.reason: Counts keys in a JS object using Object.keys; pure JS.

entries.name: attrsKeys
entries.reason: Returns Object.keys of a JS object; pure JS.

entries.name: mergeAttrs
entries.reason: Merges two attrs Records using spread; pure JS object operation.

entries.name: pipe
entries.reason: Applies an arbitrary JS function to a value; callback semantics make WASM acceleration impossible without JS callback round-trips.

entries.name: seriesApply
entries.reason: Maps an arbitrary JS function over Series elements. Each invocation crosses the JS/WASM boundary per element; net cost would exceed benefit.

entries.name: seriesTransform
entries.reason: Same as seriesApply; accepts a JS callback that must run in the JS context.

entries.name: dataFrameApply
entries.reason: Applies a JS callback across DataFrame rows/columns. Callback semantics prevent WASM acceleration.

entries.name: dataFrameApplyMap
entries.reason: Element-wise JS callback across DataFrame cells; same boundary-crossing argument.

entries.name: dataFrameTransform
entries.reason: JS callback transform on DataFrame; same reasoning as dataFrameApply.

entries.name: dataFrameTransformRows
entries.reason: Row-wise JS callback transform; same reasoning.

entries.name: isScalar
entries.reason: JS runtime type predicate that checks typeof, instanceof (Date, Timedelta), and symbol properties. No WASM-friendly primitive representation.

entries.name: isListLike
entries.reason: Checks for JS iterable/array-like protocol using Symbol.iterator; JS-specific duck-typing check.

entries.name: isArrayLike
entries.reason: Checks for JS .length property; pure JS duck-typing.

entries.name: isDictLike
entries.reason: Checks for a plain JS object with key/value access; JS-specific pattern.

entries.name: isIterator
entries.reason: Checks for the JS iterator protocol (Symbol.iterator + .next); JS-specific.

entries.name: isNumber
entries.reason: typeof check; trivial JS predicate, no WASM benefit.

entries.name: isBool
entries.reason: typeof check; trivial JS predicate.

entries.name: isStringValue
entries.reason: typeof check; trivial JS predicate.

entries.name: isFloat
entries.reason: Number.isFinite check; trivial JS predicate.

entries.name: isInteger
entries.reason: Number.isInteger check; trivial JS predicate.

entries.name: isBigInt
entries.reason: typeof check; trivial JS predicate.

entries.name: isRegExp
entries.reason: instanceof RegExp check; JS-specific.

entries.name: isReCompilable
entries.reason: Checks whether a value can be used as a RegExp source; JS-specific.

entries.name: isMissing
entries.reason: null/undefined check; trivial JS predicate.

entries.name: isHashable
entries.reason: Checks whether a value can be used as a JS Map key; JS-specific identity semantics.

entries.name: isDate
entries.reason: instanceof Date check; JS-specific.

entries.name: isNumericDtype
entries.reason: Checks whether a Dtype JS class instance represents a numeric kind; depends on JS class identity.

entries.name: isIntegerDtype
entries.reason: Dtype JS class instance check; same reasoning as isNumericDtype.

entries.name: isSignedIntegerDtype
entries.reason: Dtype JS class instance check.

entries.name: isUnsignedIntegerDtype
entries.reason: Dtype JS class instance check.

entries.name: isFloatDtype
entries.reason: Dtype JS class instance check.

entries.name: isBoolDtype
entries.reason: Dtype JS class instance check.

entries.name: isStringDtype
entries.reason: Dtype JS class instance check.

entries.name: isDatetimeDtype
entries.reason: Dtype JS class instance check.

entries.name: isTimedeltaDtype
entries.reason: Dtype JS class instance check.

entries.name: isCategoricalDtype
entries.reason: Dtype JS class instance check.

entries.name: isObjectDtype
entries.reason: Dtype JS class instance check.

entries.name: isComplexDtype
entries.reason: Dtype JS class instance check.

entries.name: isExtensionArrayDtype
entries.reason: Dtype JS class instance check using instanceof with ExtensionDtype.

entries.name: isPeriodDtype
entries.reason: Dtype JS class instance check.

entries.name: isIntervalDtype
entries.reason: Dtype JS class instance check.

entries.name: astypeSeries
entries.reason: Type-casts a Series using a Dtype JS instance; depends on JS class hierarchy, optional custom converters (JS callbacks), and returns a new Series instance.

entries.name: astype
entries.reason: Same as astypeSeries; additionally handles DataFrame column-wise coercions.

entries.name: castScalar
entries.reason: Casts a single Scalar value to a target Dtype; the cast table is defined by JS Dtype class instances and includes JS Date and BigInt conversions.

entries.name: ExtensionDtype
entries.reason: Abstract JS base class for the extension type system. Instances are registered in a runtime JS Map. No numeric kernel; purely a JS polymorphism mechanism.

entries.name: ExtensionArray
entries.reason: Abstract JS base class for extension array types. Subclasses implement custom JS accessors. No WASM-accelerable kernel.

entries.name: registerExtensionDtype
entries.reason: Registers a JS class constructor in a runtime registry Map; pure JS dynamic dispatch mechanism.

entries.name: constructExtensionDtypeFromString
entries.reason: Looks up a registered ExtensionDtype constructor by string name and calls it; JS runtime dispatch.

entries.name: registerSeriesAccessor
entries.reason: Installs a JS getter on the Series prototype; JS runtime prototype mutation.

entries.name: registerDataFrameAccessor
entries.reason: Same as registerSeriesAccessor for DataFrame.

entries.name: registerIndexAccessor
entries.reason: Same as registerSeriesAccessor for Index.

entries.name: getRegisteredAccessors
entries.reason: Returns the current state of the accessor registry (a JS Map); pure JS runtime introspection.

entries.name: apiExtensions
entries.reason: Namespace object bundling the extension API; not a function, purely a JS namespace accessor.

entries.name: Flags
entries.reason: JS class whose instances are WeakMap-backed runtime objects that track read-only/copy-on-write metadata for DataFrame/Series. Behaviour is defined by JS object identity and WeakMap lifecycle; cannot be serialised to WASM without losing those semantics.

entries.name: getFlags
entries.reason: Retrieves a Flags instance from a WeakMap keyed on a live JS object reference. Depends entirely on JS object identity and garbage-collector lifecycle; not a numeric kernel.

entries.name: MaskedArray
entries.reason: Base nullable extension array class. Its API accepts JS callbacks, returns new class instances, and integrates with the extension registry and the JS dtype system. Class-level porting would require replacing the entire TS class hierarchy.

entries.name: IntegerArray
entries.reason: Nullable integer extension array class extending MaskedArray. Same JS class-instance semantics as MaskedArray; no standalone pure numeric kernel separable from the class.

entries.name: FloatingArray
entries.reason: Nullable floating-point extension array class extending MaskedArray. Same JS class-instance semantics as MaskedArray.

entries.name: BooleanArray
entries.reason: Nullable boolean extension array class extending MaskedArray. Same JS class-instance semantics as MaskedArray.

entries.name: StringArray
entries.reason: Nullable string extension array class extending MaskedArray. String data is UTF-16 JS strings; per-element copying to WASM memory would dominate any potential throughput gain.

entries.name: DatetimeArray
entries.reason: Nullable datetime extension array class extending MaskedArray. Datetime arithmetic depends on JS Date and Timestamp objects with timezone handling; no pure numeric kernel separable from the class.

entries.name: TimedeltaArray
entries.reason: Nullable timedelta extension array class extending MaskedArray. Duration arithmetic depends on JS Timedelta objects and the extension registry; no pure numeric kernel separable from the class.

entries.name: SparseArray
entries.reason: Sparse storage array class whose fill-value semantics and block pointer structure are JS-object-based. Internal coordinate/value arrays are nullable JS arrays; no WASM-representable numeric kernel independent of the class.

entries.name: SparseDtype
entries.reason: JS class used as a runtime type token for SparseArray. Instances are compared by JS object identity throughout the dtype system; cannot be serialised to/from WASM without losing identity semantics.

entries.name: getOption
entries.reason: Runtime options registry accessor. Returns a JS value (string, number, boolean, or arbitrary object) from a live in-memory JS Map. Not a numeric operation; no WASM kernel applicable.

entries.name: setOption
entries.reason: Runtime options registry mutator. Writes to a live in-memory JS Map with optional validation callbacks. Not a numeric operation; no WASM kernel applicable.

entries.name: resetOption
entries.reason: Runtime options registry reset. Restores a JS Map entry to its default value. Not a numeric operation.

entries.name: describeOption
entries.reason: Returns a human-readable description string for a registered option. Purely a JS string operation over an in-memory registry.

entries.name: optionContext
entries.reason: Returns an async Symbol-keyed context token for scoped option overrides using JS async generators. Depends on JS Symbol identity and async lifecycle; not a numeric operation.

entries.name: registerOption
entries.reason: Registers a new option in the global JS Map with a default value and optional validator callback. Registry mutation is not a numeric operation.

entries.name: options
entries.reason: A JS Proxy object that provides live read/write access to the options registry. A Proxy is a JS-only construct with no WASM equivalent.

entries.name: api
entries.reason: Namespace object exposing the pd.api surface (types, extensions, internals). It is a static JS object used for grouping sub-namespaces; not a numeric function.

entries.name: apiTypes
entries.reason: Namespace object bundling the pd.api.types type-guard utilities. It is a static JS object; not a numeric function.

entries.name: pdArray
entries.reason: Factory function that dispatches to the appropriate nullable extension array constructor based on a dtype argument. Dispatch logic depends on JS instanceof checks and the extension registry.

entries.name: PandasArray
entries.reason: JS class wrapping a plain JS array for ndarray-compatibility (pd.arrays.PandasArray). Its API is defined by JS array semantics and the extension array protocol; not a pure numeric kernel.
